message.content:
You are synthesizing a stall-investigation into ONE findings brief for Sol (the design authority) to design the solution. Here are the structured findings from three parallel investigators:

[
  {
    "area": "Compose agent — cellcog_composer.py --write run (flywheel report for task-72), investigated read-only under /home/polaris/wt/flywheel",
    "state": "PROGRESSING, not stalled. A live --write render is in flight right now: PID 230716, launched ~02:36, sampled at 00:53→01:50→02:39 elapsed with RSS climbing 123MB→171MB and STAT flipping R→Sl (sleeping on network = LLM calls in flight). 3 openrouter/glm-5.2 workers active. No report exists yet only because the composer writes the file exactly once, atomically, at the very END (publisher.publish → outputs/release/report.md), after all 26 subsections are generated + cohesion + validate_report.",
    "root_cause": "Three independent things were conflated in the symptom report. (1) \"No report written\" is EXPECTED mid-run: cellcog_composer never writes incrementally — write_report() runs bindings→planner→fact-use-ledger→26 LLM subsection calls→cohesion→validate_report, then hands nodes to a separate publisher that atomically renames report.md into the sealed 0555 dir outputs/release/ only at the end (publisher.py:_atomic_write, RELEASE=outputs/release). (2) \"cellcog not always in the tree\" = earlier attempts were killed (dry3.log/t.log/audit_run.log all end in 'Task was destroyed but it is pending!' — asyncio generators torn down on SIGKILL/Ctrl-C between phases); the current run is a fresh, clean one via run_write.sh. (3) The agent switched inputs: the 5,759-card outputs/evidence_cards_full.json → dedup (5,369, evidence_cards_full_dedup.json) → CURATED down to 838 cards (cards_curated.json), and it is composing the 838-card curated file, NOT the 5,759 full file the task named.",
    "evidence": "Q2 — \"Positive controls: REGRESSED\": fab_base.log and fab_mine.log are BYTE-IDENTICAL and are NOT the composer — they are a separate SOL-ladder Rung2/3a fabrication-test harness driving report_ast's validator. P0/P1 positive controls (legitimate findings that MUST ship) are being REJECTED with 'SOURCE_POLICY_REFUSES: no positive identity verdict is on these bytes (None is not in the proven allowlist) — fail closed, name nothing'. This is the source-eligibility firewall (Rank12 commit 1cdd0c8) failing closed because no venue/identity allowlist is wired in — the exact report_ast facet-wiring bug the agent was sent to fix. It is NOT hard-blocking the render (the live run already passed setup into generation), but it is the same gate that zeroes out adjudication: every compose log shows 'sound cross-source verdicts placed: 0' EVEN on cards_curated where 840 comparison bundles are found — i.e. bundle FINDING is fixed (0→840) but verdict PLACEMENT is still 0 because this source-policy gate rejects the identity verdict. So it silently guts the cellcog-style cross-source analysis (the whole scoring thesis) without stopping publication. Q3 — bindings re-verify: on evidence_cards_full_dedup.json (dry/dry2.log) 5294 admitted / 2572 refused, refusals are CARD_IS_UNBOUND on CORROBORATING pointers (no manifestation_id/content_hash/span) — a data-shape defect in the corroborator lists, NOT the primary cards; primary lane still had 5294 admitted. But that file yielded 0 comparison bundles, so the agent abandoned it. On cards_curated.json (dry3.log/write.log) it is a healthy 820 admitted / 24 refused, 840 bundles, fact-use ledger 795 narrate/78 reuse/52 backref/25 unspoken. 279 'evidence units (distinct works)' is NOT starvation (cellcog#1 used ~98 works). Net: the unbound corroborators do not starve the report; the verdict-placement gate (Q2) does. Q4 — full render: prior composer versions DID publish full reports (outputs/rank6-12/report.md and outputs/cellcog_arm/report.md, 35–70KB, dated 07-12/07-13); outputs/release/ is currently EMPTY and outputs/drafts holds only stale 07-14 05:45 drops.json/fact_use_ledger.json, so THIS run has not reached publish. dry3.log shows one prior generation that reached the full section list (all sections '0 attributed | 0 owned') before being killed. Runtime: the agent's own dashboard compose_report.py hardcodes 'historical ~48m render / ETA 48m'; cost of render = 26 sequential glm-5.2 subsection calls + contract compile + cohesion, so ~30-50m is the working estimate — no completed timing for the current build is on disk yet.",
    "recommendation": "Do NOT kill PID 230716 — it is mid-render and healthy; killing it forfeits ~40m and leaves nothing (no partial output by design). Let it reach publisher.publish and check for outputs/release/report.md. TWO caveats the parent must weigh: (a) it is composing the 838-card cards_curated.json, not the 5,759-card evidence_cards_full.json the task named — confirm that curation was intentional before trusting the score. (b) Even if it publishes, 'sound cross-source verdicts placed: 0' means the source-policy/identity gate (fab_base 'no positive identity verdict… None not in the proven allowlist') is still stripping ALL cross-source adjudication — the report will render as flat REPORTING, not the cellcog-style ANALYSIS that the whole flywheel thesis (FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS lines 174-348) says is the only lever that beats the 0.54 cluster. The real fix is to wire a positive-identity/venue allowlist into report_ast's source policy so P0/P1 controls in fab_base.log pass, which will also let verdicts be placed (0→N). The CARD_IS_UNBOUND corroborator refusals are a secondary data-hygiene item (stamp manifestation_id/content_hash/span onto corroborating entries) but are not what is blocking quality.",
    "reliable_command": "COMPOSE (already running as run_write.sh; to reproduce cleanly): cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && export PYTHONPATH=scripts:src && export PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN=100000 && set -a && source .env && set +a && export PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION=\"$(cat /home/polaris/polaris_project/task72_prompt.txt)\" && python -u scripts/cellcog_composer.py --cards <CARDS> --write   (use --cards outputs/evidence_cards_full.json for the task-named 5,759-card corpus, or the scratchpad cards_curated.json for the 838-card curated set the agent chose; add --dry to prove bindings+AST with zero token spend first). The composer prints the published path and writes it to outputs/release/report.md (dir is chmod-sealed 0555 except during the atomic rename). THEN SCORE: cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && set -a && source .env && set +a && python scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/release/report.md --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72 --race-model openai/gpt-5.5 --max-workers 4   (reads DRB/data/prompt_data/query.jsonl for task 72, writes DRB/results/race/polaris_task72/race_result.txt). Wait for the current PID 230716 to finish before re-invoking to avoid two writers racing the same release dir."
  },
  {
    "area": "glm-5.2 card-audit hang — asyncio event loop torn down per-call inside ThreadPoolExecutor workers (scripts/card_audit/glm_transport.py + orchestrator scripts/card_audit/run_audit.py, transport src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py). Files: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/card_audit/{glm_transport.py,run_audit.py,harness.py}, /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py, checkpoint /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/card_audit_checkpoint.jsonl.",
    "state": "broken-but-recoverable — the run is wedged (throughput decayed 1.8/s -> 0.95/s and stalling), but 394 rows are durably checkpointed and the orchestrator is checkpoint-resumable. (Note: your \"175/5759\" is stale; the log reached 350 and the checkpoint holds 394 rows.) The near-100% quarantine is a SEPARATE calibration problem, not caused by the hang.",
    "root_cause": "CONFIRMED. The hang is the async OpenRouterClient being driven by a throwaway event loop per call inside worker threads. glm_transport._glm_generate wraps every judge call in asyncio.run(_run()) (glm_transport.py:52-72), and run_audit.py runs 9 of these concurrently in a ThreadPoolExecutor (run_audit.py:198-201). OpenRouterClient is a fully-async, SSE-streaming client: __init__ builds an httpx.AsyncClient (openrouter_client.py:1559,1567,1595), and every call streams via `async with self._client.stream(...)` + `async for line in response.aiter_lines()` (openrouter_client.py:1642,1772) plus asyncio.timeout()/wait_for child tasks. This design assumes ONE long-lived loop. asyncio.run() instead creates a fresh loop per call, then on exit cancels pending tasks and calls loop.shutdown_asyncgens() and closes the loop — but the SSE aiter_lines async-generators (and httpx's internal ones) are still live, so their aclose()/athrow cleanup coroutines are scheduled as `async_generator_athrow` tasks and then orphaned when the loop is destroyed -> the \"Task was destroyed but it is pending!\" flood (log full of `coro=<<async_generator_athrow without __name__>()>`). Because a brand-new AsyncClient + connection pool is also built and torn down every single call and cannot gracefully drain before the loop closes, sockets/fds and connection objects leak across all 9 threads. That leak is what actually degrades and finally wedges the run (the steady rate decay is the signature); the pending-task messages are the visible symptom of the same teardown-mid-stream defect. So yes: async client + asyncio.run() inside ThreadPoolExecutor workers is the root cause.",
    "evidence": "Log /tmp/.../scratchpad/audit_run.log: hundreds of \"Task was destroyed but it is pending! task: <Task pending ... coro=<<async_generator_athrow without __name__>()>>\"; progress lines show monotonic rate decay 1.80 -> 1.55 -> 1.10 -> 0.95/s (ETA 53m climbing to 94.5m) with errors=0 — a resource-leak slowdown, not a crash. glm_transport.py:72 `return asyncio.run(_run())`; :57 constructs a new OpenRouterClient inside every call. run_audit.py:198 ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=9) submitting audit_one -> harness.audit_card -> make_glm_runner. openrouter_client.py:1642 `async for line in response.aiter_lines()`, :1772 `async with self._client.stream(...)`, :1595 httpx.AsyncClient. Checkpoint tally (outputs/card_audit_checkpoint.jsonl, 394 rows): buckets {quarantined:393, kept_unchanged:1}; reason codes dominated by DETERMINISTIC Tier-0 gates (cot.unclassified_free_text=164, cache.source_counts_mismatch=116, corroborator.incomplete_binding=116, schema.duplicate_id=55) and report-AST faithfulness.span_does_not_entail_claim=194; only opus.transport_failed_after_retries=1. 91 cards were quarantined despite faith_verdict=PASS. => the mass quarantine is Tier-0/entailment calibration, essentially independent of the transport hang.",
    "recommendation": "(1) ROOT CAUSE: replace the per-call asyncio.run() of the async client with a synchronous transport in the worker; do NOT run the async SSE client on throwaway per-call loops under a ThreadPoolExecutor. (2) USABLE OUTPUT: essentially nothing usable as an AUDITED CARD SET — only 1 of 394 cards was kept, and 91 faith-PASS cards were quarantined, so the disposition/calibration output is not trustworthy; the 394 checkpoint rows ARE usable as diagnostic data (reason-code census). Treat the run as diagnostic-only. (3) RESUMABLE: yes — run_audit._load_done_rids() reads the checkpoint and pending is recomputed by re-deriving tier0.audit_row_id('', f'/{idx}', card) per index and skipping done rids (run_audit.py:81-93,186-191); the 394 rows persist, so a rerun resumes without re-auditing them. (Rows are appended under a lock and flushed per card, so the checkpoint is intact.) (4) CORRECT TRANSPORT: a SYNCHRONOUS per-call POST inside the worker — a plain requests/httpx.Client().post() to {OPENROUTER_BASE_URL}/chat/completions with the OpenRouter key, non-streaming (or sync-iter) — returning content + usage, shaped into the same claude-p-style envelope make_glm_runner already builds (result/model/modelUsage/is_error). No asyncio, no async generators, no per-call event loop, so nothing leaks and there is no loop-teardown-mid-stream. Fix SHAPE: rewrite glm_transport._glm_generate to do one blocking sync HTTP POST (reuse a thread-local or module httpx.Client for pooling); keep make_glm_runner's retry/fail-closed and envelope logic unchanged. If the async client must be reused, the only safe alternative is one persistent event loop per worker thread (created once, reused across calls, never closed per-call) — but a sync POST is simpler and matches a CPU-light judge call. Separately, before any real rerun, investigate the Tier-0/entailment over-quarantine (the glm-5.2 strict-entailment judge and the cot/cache/corroborator gates) since that, not the hang, is what makes the output unusable.",
    "reliable_command": "To resume cleanly AFTER swapping _glm_generate to a synchronous httpx/requests POST (do not run as-is — it will re-wedge): cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && python3 scripts/card_audit/run_audit.py --workers 9   (it auto-skips the 394 checkpointed rows). To rebuild reports from the existing checkpoint without any LLM calls: python3 scripts/card_audit/run_audit.py --report-only"
  },
  {
    "area": "compose→score pipeline readiness (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel, branch flywheel-v1) — NOT the cwd /home/polaris/polaris_project, where none of these files exist",
    "state": "recoverable — pipeline is READY for a clean direct run after ONE required pre-step (dedup the cards file). All other components verified green. No project files were modified.",
    "root_cause": "outputs/evidence_cards_full.json (5,759 cards) is NOT consumable as-is: it holds only 5,369 distinct ids — 299 duplicate id values / 390 extra cards. CardBundle.__init__ (scripts/report_ast.py:293-308) REFUSES any duplicate id with ValueError, so cellcog_composer.py crashes at load_bundle before any LLM call. Deduping keep-first to 5,369 cards fixes it: the bundle then constructs and yields 5,294 admitted / 279 works. The composer's own default --cards is outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json (only 232 cards), so the full-corpus run MUST pass --cards explicitly at a deduped file.",
    "evidence": "(1) evidence_cards_full.json: 5,759 cards, 5,369 distinct ids, 299 dup id values (390 extra); all 5,759 carry manifestation_id/content_hash/span_start/span_end. (2) Built CardBundle from a keep-first dedup (5,369) against provenance_graph.json: constructs OK, admitted_ids()=5,294, refused=75 (ALL 'EXPRESSION_KIND_IS_NOT_RENDERABLE_AS_A_CITATION'), evidence works among admitted=279 — matches the task's '5294 admitted / 279 units' exactly. (3) provenance_graph.json (28MB) strict-loads via P.Graph.from_json: 501 works, 437 manifestations, 637 expressions. (4) _facet_contract() at report_ast.py:1488 calls research_contract.compile_contract(question, use_llm=True, verbose=False), gated on PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION with fail-safe to AP.default_contract(); report_ast, research_contract, cellcog_composer all import cleanly. (5) write_report (cellcog_composer.py:939-953): bad=reverify(b) is printed but NEVER blocks — only 'if not admitted: return 1' halts; so the 2,572 refused corroborators are cosmetic, composition runs on the 5,294 admitted primary bindings. (6) score_report_race.py reads third_party/deep_research_bench/data/prompt_data/query.jsonl; task 72 present (lang en, AI-labor-market review); reference.jsonl + criteria_data/criteria.jsonl (id 72) + deepresearch_bench_race.py all present; requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY, judge openai/gpt-5.5 (RACE_MODEL default). (7) composer generator = OpenRouterClient(model=PG_GENERATOR_MODEL default z-ai/glm-5.2) → also needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY. (8) publisher writes the judged artifact to outputs/release/report.md. (9) .env → /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.env, contains OPENROUTER_API_KEY. policy 'journal_articles_only' maps to P.JOURNAL_ONLY.",
    "recommendation": "A clean run needs exactly one fix: dedup outputs/evidence_cards_full.json to unique ids (keep first) and pass it via --cards; do NOT rely on the composer default (evidence_cards_bound.json = 232 cards). Do NOT try to 'repair' the 2,572 refused corroborators or the 75 unrenderable-expression cards — neither blocks; the composer intentionally composes from the 5,294 admitted primaries. Set PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION to the exact task-72 prompt so the compiled contract drives title/abstract/facets to the benchmark subject (otherwise the frame degrades to a subject-free generic and cross-source verdicts thin out). Optionally run a no-LLM preflight first: `--dry` with PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION UNSET proves every binding + the full AST against the graph bytes without spending a token and without touching outputs/release. A residual to watch (not blocking): outputs/event_ledger.jsonl (11MB) is only parsed later inside _fact_use_plan during --write, not at load — the --dry preflight does exercise it.",
    "reliable_command": "cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel\nset -a && . ./.env && set +a                      # OPENROUTER_API_KEY (+ RACE_MODEL default openai/gpt-5.5)\nDEDUP=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/evidence_cards_full_dedup.json\n# 1) DEDUP (299 dup ids / 390 extra cards WILL crash CardBundle) -> 5,369 unique -> 5,294 admitted / 279 works\npython3 - \"$DEDUP\" <<'PY'\nimport json,sys\ncards=json.load(open('outputs/evidence_cards_full.json')); seen=set(); out=[]\nfor c in cards:\n    i=c['id']\n    if i in seen: continue\n    seen.add(i); out.append(c)\njson.dump(out, open(sys.argv[1],'w')); print('dedup wrote', len(out), 'cards')\nPY\n# 2) OPTIONAL no-LLM preflight (proves bindings+AST, leaves outputs/release untouched): PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION unset\npython -u scripts/cellcog_composer.py --cards \"$DEDUP\" --graph outputs/provenance_graph.json --ledger outputs/event_ledger.jsonl --policy journal_articles_only --dry\n# 3) COMPOSE (calls OpenRouter: contract-compile + per-subsection generation; publishes outputs/release/report.md)\nexport PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION=\"Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.\"\nexport PG_GENERATOR_MODEL=\"z-ai/glm-5.2\"           # optional; this is already the default\npython -u scripts/cellcog_composer.py --cards \"$DEDUP\" --graph outputs/provenance_graph.json --ledger outputs/event_ledger.jsonl --policy journal_articles_only --write\n# 4) SCORE with RACE (task 72; judge openai/gpt-5.5 via OpenRouter; needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY from step 1)\npython scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/release/report.md --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72"
  }
]

Write a concise, decision-ready brief (markdown) covering: (1) COMPOSE — is it recoverable or should we run it clean directly; the exact reliable command; any data/regression blocker. (2) AUDIT — root cause of the asyncio hang and the correct fix shape; whether to fix-and-resume or defer. (3) The RELIABLE PATH to a scored report (the deliverable the operator wants tonight), step by step with exact commands. (4) Open questions Sol must decide. Save it by printing it in full. Be concrete — this goes straight to Sol.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
# STALL INVESTIGATION — Decision Brief for Sol

**Target:** one scored flywheel report for task-72 tonight.
**Repo:** all work is in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel` (branch `flywheel-v1`) — NOT the cwd `/home/polaris/polaris_project`.
**Bottom line:** Nothing is fatally stalled. There is a live, healthy compose run in flight and a verified clean-run path. The only real quality blocker is a source-policy gate that zeroes cross-source verdicts. Audit hang is real but OFF tonight's critical path.

---

## 1) COMPOSE — recoverable; a live run is already mid-render

**State: PROGRESSING, not stalled.** `cellcog_composer.py --write` is live as **PID 230716** (launched ~02:36, RSS climbing 123→171MB, STAT R→Sl = sleeping on LLM calls, 3 glm-5.2 workers active). "No report yet" is EXPECTED: the composer never writes incrementally — it generates all 26 subsections → cohesion → validate → then a single atomic publish to `outputs/release/report.md` at the very end. ETA ~30–50m from launch (the dashboard hardcodes ~48m).

**Two blockers/caveats — both material:**

- **(a) Wrong corpus (data).** The live run is composing the **838-card `cards_curated.json`**, NOT the **5,759-card `outputs/evidence_cards_full.json`** the task named. The agent curated: full (5,759) → dedup (5,369) → curated (838). 279 works is not starvation (cellcog#1 used ~98), so it will still produce a report — but it is not the corpus the task specified. **Sol must decide if that curation was intentional.**

- **(b) Cross-source verdicts = 0 (regression, non-blocking to publish, fatal to quality).** Every compose log shows `sound cross-source verdicts placed: 0` even though bundle-finding is fixed (0→840 comparison bundles). The **source-eligibility firewall (Rank12, commit 1cdd0c8)** is failing closed: `SOURCE_POLICY_REFUSES: no positive identity verdict… (None is not in the proven allowlist)`. No venue/identity allowlist is wired into `report_ast`, so it strips ALL cross-source adjudication. The report will publish as **flat REPORTING, not the cellcog-style ANALYSIS** that FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS says is the only lever that beats the 0.54 cluster. This is the exact facet-wiring bug the agent was originally sent to fix. The P0/P1 positive controls in `fab_base.log`/`fab_mine.log` (byte-identical SOL-ladder harness, not the composer) are being rejected by this same gate.

- **(c) Load crash on the FULL corpus (data, if you re-run clean).** `evidence_cards_full.json` has 5,759 cards but only 5,369 distinct ids (299 dup ids / 390 extra). `CardBundle.__init__` raises `ValueError` on any duplicate id → composer crashes at load. **Must dedup keep-first → 5,369** (yields 5,294 admitted / 279 works). The composer's default `--cards` is `evidence_cards_bound.json` (only 232 cards) — never rely on it; always pass `--cards` explicitly.

**Recommendation:** Do **not** kill PID 230716 (killing forfeits ~40m and leaves nothing — no partial output by design). Let it publish, then judge whether the 838-card + verdicts-0 output is good enough. If Sol wants the task-named corpus and/or real adjudication, run clean per §3 **after** the live run finishes (never two writers on the sealed `outputs/release/` at once).

---

## 2) AUDIT (glm-5.2 card-audit) — CONFIRMED root cause; DEFER for tonight

**Root cause (confirmed):** `glm_transport._glm_generate` wraps every judge call in `asyncio.run(_run())` (glm_transport.py:52-72) and constructs a **new async `OpenRouterClient` per call**, run 9-wide in a `ThreadPoolExecutor` (run_audit.py:198). `OpenRouterClient` is a fully-async SSE-streaming client (`async with self._client.stream(...)` + `async for line in aiter_lines()`). `asyncio.run()` creates a throwaway loop per call and closes it while the SSE async-generators are still live → orphaned `async_generator_athrow` cleanup tasks ("Task was destroyed but it is pending!" flood) and a brand-new AsyncClient + connection pool built/torn down every call that can't drain → **socket/fd leak across all 9 threads → monotonic rate decay (1.80→0.95/s) until wedged.** Errors=0; it's a resource-leak slowdown, not a crash.

**Correct fix shape:** Replace the per-call `asyncio.run()` of the async client with a **synchronous, non-streaming HTTP POST** inside the worker — a module/thread-local `httpx.Client().post()` to `{OPENROUTER_BASE_URL}/chat/completions`, returning content+usage shaped into the same envelope `make_glm_runner` already builds. Keep the retry/fail-closed/envelope logic unchanged. (Fallback if the async client must be reused: one persistent loop per worker thread, created once, never closed per-call — but a sync POST is simpler and matches a CPU-light judge call.)

**Fix-and-resume or defer? DEFER.** Two reasons:
1. It is **not on tonight's compose→score path** — compose reads the curated/full cards, not the audit output.
2. The audit output is **unusable anyway**: only 1 of 394 cards kept, 91 faith-PASS cards quarantined. That is a **separate Tier-0/entailment over-quarantine calibration problem**, independent of the hang. Fixing the transport gets you a fast run of a mis-calibrated judge.
- Run is checkpoint-resumable (394 rows durable in `outputs/card_audit_checkpoint.jsonl`; `--report-only` rebuilds reports with zero LLM calls). The 394 rows are good **diagnostic data** (reason-code census), not a trustworthy audited card set.

---

## 3) RELIABLE PATH TO A SCORED REPORT (the deliverable)

**Option A — ride the live run (fastest, if 838-card corpus is acceptable):**
```bash
# Watch for publish (PID 230716 writes exactly once, atomically, at the end):
ls -l /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/release/report.md
# When it exists, SCORE it:
cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && set -a && source .env && set +a
python scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/release/report.md \
  --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72 --race-model openai/gpt-5.5 --max-workers 4
# → writes DRB/results/race/polaris_task72/race_result.txt
```

**Option B — clean run on the task-named 5,759-card corpus (do this if Sol rejects the curation; run only AFTER PID 230716 finishes):**
```bash
cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
set -a && . ./.env && set +a                        # OPENROUTER_API_KEY + RACE_MODEL default

# 1) DEDUP full corpus (299 dup ids WILL crash CardBundle) → 5,369 unique
DEDUP=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/evidence_cards_full_dedup.json
python3 - "$DEDUP" <<'PY'
import json,sys
cards=json.load(open('outputs/evidence_cards_full.json')); seen=set(); out=[]
for c in cards:
    i=c['id']
    if i in seen: continue
    seen.add(i); out.append(c)
json.dump(out, open(sys.argv[1],'w')); print('dedup wrote', len(out), 'cards')
PY

# 2) OPTIONAL no-token preflight (proves bindings+AST, leaves release/ untouched; PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION UNSET)
python -u scripts/cellcog_composer.py --cards "$DEDUP" \
  --graph outputs/provenance_graph.json --ledger outputs/event_ledger.jsonl \
  --policy journal_articles_only --dry

# 3) COMPOSE + PUBLISH  (set the EXACT task-72 prompt so the contract frames to the benchmark subject)
export PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION="$(cat /home/polaris/polaris_project/task72_prompt.txt)"
export PG_GENERATOR_MODEL="z-ai/glm-5.2"            # already the default
python -u scripts/cellcog_composer.py --cards "$DEDUP" \
  --graph outputs/provenance_graph.json --ledger outputs/event_ledger.jsonl \
  --policy journal_articles_only --write

# 4) SCORE
python scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/release/report.md \
  --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72 --race-model openai/gpt-5.5 --max-workers 4
```

**Guardrails:** never run two `--write` composers against the sealed `outputs/release/` (0555, unsealed only during atomic rename) simultaneously. Always pass `--cards` explicitly (default is the 232-card `evidence_cards_bound.json`). Always set `PG_RESEARCH_QUESTION` to the exact task-72 prompt or the frame degrades to subject-free generic and verdicts thin further.

**Note:** Neither option fixes the verdicts=0 gate (§4). Both will produce a *publishable, scorable* report tonight; whether it *beats the 0.54 cluster* depends on the gate fix.
